message:
fix(bitbucket,gitlab): percent-encode path segments in REST client URLs

Both the Bitbucket Cloud v2 client and the GitLab v4 client were
interpolating caller-supplied identifiers (`workspace`, `repo_slug`,
`project_id`, `pr_id`, etc.) directly into URL path templates via
`format!`. Any identifier containing `/`, spaces, `?`, `#`, or other
reserved characters would silently reshape the request path — breaking
legitimate calls (GitLab namespace project IDs like `group/subgroup/repo`
are particularly exposed, since the native URL form *is* `group%2Fsubgroup%2Frepo`)
and, in adversarial cases, enabling path-traversal style URL injection
(a value of `../../foo` would escape the intended endpoint).

Fix:

- connectors/bitbucket/src/client.rs
- connectors/gitlab/src/client.rs

  Introduce a single `encode_path_segment` helper per crate, backed by
  `percent_encoding::utf8_percent_encode` over a custom `AsciiSet` that
  only keeps the RFC 3986 unreserved characters (alphanumerics, `-`,
  `_`, `.`, `~`). Slashes are deliberately encoded so a namespace path
  survives as a single opaque segment. All list/get/create helpers
  (`list_repositories`, `get_repository`, `list_pull_requests`,
  `get_pull_request`, `create_pull_request`, `list_branches`,
  `list_commits`, `list_pipelines` on Bitbucket; the analogous GitLab
  surface) now route their inputs through the helper before
  interpolation.

  Unit tests added next to the helper cover the four canonical cases:
  simple slugs untouched, slashes encoded, spaces encoded, and generic
  special characters (?, =) encoded.

- connectors/bitbucket/Cargo.toml
- connectors/gitlab/Cargo.toml
  Add `percent-encoding.workspace = true` so the helper compiles
  without pulling an arbitrary version.

- Cargo.lock
  Regenerated to reflect the two new per-crate dependency edges.

No connector-facing behavior changes for already-correct inputs — the
unreserved set is preserved exactly, so existing callers producing
clean slugs see byte-identical URLs.

files.patch:
@@ -25,6 +25,7 @@ fcp-async-core = { path = "../../crates/fcp-async-core" }
 fcp-sdk = { path = "../../crates/fcp-sdk" }
 
 anyhow.workspace = true
+percent-encoding.workspace = true
 async-trait.workspace = true
 reqwest.workspace = true
 serde.workspace = true
